title: 26 openers, redrafted with teeth
intro: Round-2 verdict was more punchy — every opener is redrafted with command-form hooks, tension, and loss-framing (hype adjectives still banned). Each card shows my pick, two alternates, and the round-1 line; approve, or name any card where you prefer an alternate. The opener doubles as the GitHub About text, package description, docs hero tagline, and banner tagline. Nothing lands until you approve.

submit.label: Approve openers
submit.note: Approving green-lights the retrofit: README rewrites, demo media, and gh repo edit --description, pushed direct to main.

blocks.title: Flagged — corrected before approval
blocks.md: Both repos are pre-release; their drafted install commands do not work today. Corrections are shown inline, and the get-started block is held until the plugin ships.

blocks.title: cc-factory
blocks.summary: An assembly line of agents that plan, build, review, and ship your spec.

children.md: A software factory for Claude Code: orchestrated agents that plan, build, review, and ship software.

**Your spec clocks in. A reviewed diff clocks out.**

cc-factory is an assembly line of agents: your spec gets designed, planned, built in parallel, reviewed, and argued against before it leaves the floor.

children.prompt: My pick is selected. Prefer an alternate?

options.label: Your spec clocks in. A reviewed diff clocks out.
Stop reviewing your agent's first draft.
Your spec runs the line: design, plan, parallel build, review, pushback.

children.md: **Correction:** the repo is docs-only — no `.claude-plugin/` manifest exists, so the install commands (also in today's README) fail. The retrofit keeps the opener but adds an honest **Status: design stage** line and defers the install/agent block and demo until the plugin ships.

children.prompt: Approve this opener and the deferred-install correction?

blocks.title: Tier A — repos with docs sites
blocks.md: README, docs landing, and walkthrough all get the treatment.

blocks.title: captain-hook
blocks.summary: Turns the corrections you keep giving Claude into typed, tested hooks.

children.md: Guardrails for Claude Code, written as typed, testable data — and learned from the corrections you give Claude.

**Stop repeating yourself to Claude.**

captain-hook mines your transcripts for the corrections you keep giving and opens PRs that turn each one into a typed, tested Python hook.

children.prompt: My pick is selected. Prefer an alternate?

options.label: Claude isn't done until the tests pass.
All the autonomy. None of the rm -rf.

blocks.title: cc-review
blocks.summary: Renders your working tree as a local PR and freezes edits until you Submit.

children.md: Local code-review daemon + Claude plugin — review what Claude writes in a PR-like web UI and stream feedback back.

**The only thing between Claude and its next edit is your Submit button.**

cc-review renders your working tree as a local PR page and streams your comments to Claude live; a PreToolUse hook freezes edits until you press Submit.

children.prompt: My pick is selected. Prefer an alternate?

options.label: The only thing between Claude and its next edit is your Submit button.
Stop reviewing Claude's diffs in terminal scrollback.
Inline-comment a commit that doesn't exist yet.

blocks.title: slop-cop
blocks.summary: 48 detectors that catch the tells of LLM-written prose, in CI or inline.

children.md: A Go CLI that detects the rhetorical and structural tells of LLM-generated prose and emits a structured JSON report.

**Never ship 'delve' again.**

slop-cop reads your agent's prose with 48 detectors and returns a JSON rap sheet the agent revises against, in CI, pre-commit, or inline.

children.prompt: My pick is selected. Prefer an alternate?

options.label: Your agent's prose, booked on 48 counts of slop.

children.title: Get started
children.code:
brew install yasyf/tap/slop-cop
slop-cop check draft.md --pretty

blocks.title: cc-notes
blocks.summary: Agent tasks and notes as a git op-log, invisible to checkout and diff.

children.md: Notes and tasks for AI agents, stored as objects in your repo's git database — no server, no sidecar, invisible in checkouts.

**Delete your HANDOFF.md.**

cc-notes keeps agent tasks, notes, and docs as a CRDT op-log on refs/cc-notes/*, synced over plain git and invisible to checkout and diff.

children.prompt: My pick is selected. Prefer an alternate?

options.label: Your agents remember. Your diff never knows.
Never re-explain the plan to a fresh agent.

blocks.title: cc-interact
blocks.summary: The reusable daemon, event log, SSE, and MCP plumbing behind cc-review.

children.md: A domain-agnostic framework for human-in-the-loop Claude agents — daemon, event stream, MCP channel, and optional web UI.

**Never write a daemon for a Claude Code tool again.**

cc-interact extracts cc-review's daemon, event log, SSE, edit-gate, and MCP plumbing into a reusable Go framework; the echo example is 381 lines.

children.prompt: My pick is selected. Prefer an alternate?

options.label: Never write a daemon for a Claude Code tool again.
An echo server with a daemon, event log, SSE, edit gate, and MCP is 381 lines.
All of cc-review's plumbing. None of the review.
